title: Routing, Selection, Translation, and Redundancy · Fault Triage
summary: Connect branch, headquarters, cloud, and internet routes while diagnosing asymmetry and failover. Learners isolate plausible failures, compare competing hypotheses, and record cause-level repair evidence. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a routed hybrid branch design and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Read connected, static, dynamic, default, and host routes and select the longest matching destination prefix.
Configure and evaluate static and default routes with next-hop reachability, recursion, failover, and documentation.
Compare BGP, EIGRP, and OSPF scope, neighbor behavior, path information, convergence, and use cases at N10-009 depth.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# comptia-network-plus safe operations evidence notebook
# Commands run only in the deterministic browser simulator.
# Add one observation, command, expected signal, and conclusion per activity.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise

steps.title: Publish the incident record
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Net Subinterfaces applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use router or firewall subinterfaces with 802.1Q tags and gateway addresses for segmented routing.
